Petition For Authority To List And Sell Real Property; To Enter Into An Exclusive Listing Agreement With Real Estate Agent; And Petition To Authorize A Preliminary Distribution To Trust Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi for and with Barbara De Vries (Petitioner/Successor Trustee).
Eric Miller for Elaine Whitney (Beneficiary).
Vincent Yu for Eric Zero.

Mr. Yu stated that he filed his objections yesterday, and that he attempted to contact Attorney Yvette Davis to request the file and to talk about what she has done with Mr. Zero, but she has not responded back to them. Ms. Alavi stated that the objections make absolutely no sense, that they are a response to a complaint, that this is a petition to sell the property, that the affirmative actions listed go from the first, to the second and to the seventh, and that Mr. Yu failed to list one single factual information as to what the objection is. When asked by the Court, Mr. Yu stated that he has not filed a substitution of attorney. The Court informed Mr. Yu that his objection will not be accepted as he is not the Attorney of record. Ms. Alavi stated that she and her Client spent a number of months working with Ms. Davis to allow Mr. Zero the opportunity to obtain a loan, that Mr. Zero is not the only Beneficiary, that Mr. Zero's Sister is not interested in being a co-owner with him, that there are $190,000.00 in liens which are accruing interest and that there is no money in the trust to pay the liens. Ms. Alavi also stated that when Mr. Zero was unable to obtain a loan, Ms. Davis said she was in agreement to sell the property, that Mr. Zero was managing the property and moved in to the owners unit, and that his employment was terminated so that the owner's unit could be vacant and yield a higher sale. Ms. De Vries stated that Mr. Zero has no work history, no assets and no income to show a lender, and that the other Attorney tried to secure a loan for Mr. Zero, but the could not do it. Mr. Yu stated that they have not attempted to get a loan and that he wanted to talk with Ms. Davis first. Mr. Miller stated that he received an e-mail back in October of 2018 indicating that there was no loan and that they agreed to sell, and that this is a hardship on his Client. The Court granted the Petition for Authority to List and Sell Real Property, etc. (subject to review of the order).
Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
